allies.rationale_text: Circle-of-competence prevents deliberate-practice from being misapplied to problems outside one's domain — knowing your competence boundary ensures practice targets the right skill area instead of entrenching the most available framework.
allies.source_quote: Practice intellectual humility—acknowledging what you don't know and staying within a well-defined circle of competence. If less believable, start by adopting the role of a student.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops make deliberate-practice real by converting reps into correction, comparison, and visible improvement.
allies.source_quote: Deliberate Practice thrives within environments that provide constant, high-quality feedback.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking supports deliberate-practice by helping the practitioner see how sub-skills, benchmarks, and feedback conditions fit together as a capability-building system.
allies.source_quote: Systems Thinking and Mental Models: DP is the route to developing fluency in mental models from major academic disciplines.

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias turns deliberate-practice into routine repetition by preserving familiar drills after the evidence says stretch is needed.
antagonists.source_quote: deliberate-practice conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when routine repetition replaces feedback-driven skill stretch.

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-bias keeps practitioners attached to ineffective drills simply because they have already invested in them.
antagonists.source_quote: deliberate-practice conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when practitioners persist with ineffective drills despite evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Deliberate-practice conflicts with status-quo-bias when routine repetition replaces feedback-driven skill stretch.
structured_tensions.source_quote: deliberate-practice vs status-quo-bias: deliberate-practice conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when routine repetition replaces feedback-driven skill stretch.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Deliberate-practice conflicts with commitment-bias when practitioners persist with ineffective drills despite evidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: deliberate-practice vs commitment-bias: deliberate-practice conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when practitioners persist with ineffective drills despite evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Deliberate-practice conflicts with confirmation-bias when entrenched practice reinforces an already flawed mental model rather than exposing it to corrective feedback.
structured_tensions.source_quote: deliberate-practice vs confirmation-bias: deliberate-practice conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when entrenched practice reinforces an already flawed mental model rather than exposing it to corrective feedback.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on feedback-linked practice and an explicit tension block around routine drift, sunk drills, and model entrenchment.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor training-program relation sprawl beyond the strongest feedback and anti-entrenchment links.
open_questions: Circle-of-competence wired as ally (Task 4.2 sweep) — explicit in risks/mitigations section.
